description: Add MCP tools for debugging the Chrome extension internals: comprehensive state inspection, internal log collection, side panel visibility, adapter injection diagnostics, and soft reconnection.

userStories.title: Add internal log collector to background and offscreen scripts
userStories.description: As a developer debugging the extension, I want internal logs from the background script and offscreen document to be captured in a ring buffer so that the extension_get_logs tool (US-003) can return them without requiring manual DevTools access.
acceptanceCriteria: New file platform/browser-extension/src/log-collector.ts exports a singleton LogCollector class

LogCollector wraps console.log, console.warn, console.error, and console.info — intercepting calls, storing entries in a ring buffer, AND still calling the original console methods so DevTools output is not lost
Each log entry stores: timestamp (ms), level ('log' | 'warn' | 'error' | 'info'), message (formatted string), and source ('background' | 'offscreen' | 'side-panel')
Ring buffer holds a configurable max entries (default 500) — oldest entries are dropped when full
LogCollector provides getEntries(options) method: optional filters for level, source, since (timestamp), and limit; returns entries newest-first
LogCollector provides clear() method to flush the buffer
LogCollector provides getStats() returning { totalCaptured, bufferSize, oldestTimestamp, newestTimestamp }
Background script (background.ts) imports and initializes the log collector at startup with source='background'
Offscreen document (offscreen/index.ts) imports and initializes its own instance with source='offscreen'
Log entries are serializable (no circular references, functions, or DOM nodes — all arguments are stringified)
Individual log messages are truncated at 2000 characters to prevent memory issues from large object dumps

userStories.title: Add extension_get_state tool for comprehensive state inspection
userStories.description: As an AI agent debugging the extension, I want a single tool that returns the complete internal state of the extension so that I can quickly understand the overall health: WebSocket connection, all plugins with their tab states, adapter injection status, active network captures, and pending operations.
acceptanceCriteria: New file platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/extension-get-state.ts defines the extension_get_state tool using defineBrowserTool
Tool accepts no required parameters (inspects the extension itself, not a tab)
Tool dispatches to extension via 'extension.getState' method
Extension handler in browser-commands.ts implements handleExtensionGetState
Returned state includes connection: { wsConnected, mcpServerUrl }
Returned state includes plugins: array of { name, version, displayName, urlPatterns, toolCount, tabState, matchingTabId }
Returned state includes networkCaptures: array of { tabId, requestCount, isCapturing } for active sessions
Returned state includes offscreen: { exists } — checked via chrome.runtime.getContexts
'extension.getState' added to dispatch table in message-router.ts
userStories.notes:
This handler runs in the background script context, so it has direct access to in-memory state. Key data sources:

1. Connection: Read wsConnected flag from background.ts. Need to export it or provide a getter. Read mcpServerUrl from chrome.storage.local.
2. Plugins: Call getAllPluginMeta() from plugin-storage.ts. For tab state, import the lastKnownState Map from tab-state.ts (need to export it or add a getter like getLastKnownStates()).
3. Network captures: Import isCapturing and add a new export getActiveCaptureTabIds() from network-capture.ts.
4. Offscreen document: Use chrome.runtime.getContexts({ contextTypes: ['OFFSCREEN_DOCUMENT'] }) to check if it exists.

Files to create/edit: (1) Create platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/extension-get-state.ts, (2) Add to browser-tools/index.ts, (3) Add handleExtensionGetState to browser-commands.ts, (4) Add 'extension.getState' to message-router.ts + ALLOWED_METHODS, (5) Add state getter exports to tab-state.ts, network-capture.ts.

userStories.title: Add extension_get_logs tool for internal log retrieval
userStories.description: As an AI agent debugging extension issues, I want to retrieve internal logs from the background script and offscreen document so that I can see error messages, WebSocket events, and plugin injection warnings without opening DevTools.
acceptanceCriteria: New file platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/extension-get-logs.ts defines the extension_get_logs tool using defineBrowserTool

Tool accepts optional parameters: level (filter by 'log' | 'warn' | 'error' | 'info' | 'all'), source (filter by 'background' | 'offscreen' | 'all'), limit (max entries, defaults to 100), since (timestamp)
Tool dispatches to extension via 'extension.getLogs' method
Extension handler combines logs from background LogCollector and offscreen LogCollector (via bg:getLogs message)
Returns { entries: Array<{ timestamp, level, source, message }>, stats: { totalBackground, totalOffscreen, bufferSize } }
Entries are sorted by timestamp descending (newest first)
'extension.getLogs' added to dispatch table in message-router.ts
userStories.notes:
Depends on US-001 (log collector). The handler needs to:
1. Get background logs directly from the local LogCollector instance
2. Get offscreen logs by sending chrome.runtime.sendMessage with type 'bg:getLogs'
3. Merge the two arrays by timestamp
4. Apply filters (level, source, since, limit)
5. Return the result

Files to create/edit: (1) Create browser-tools/extension-get-logs.ts, (2) Add to browser-tools/index.ts, (3) Add handleExtensionGetLogs to browser-commands.ts, (4) Add 'extension.getLogs' to message-router.ts + ALLOWED_METHODS.

userStories.title: Add extension_get_side_panel_state tool for side panel inspection
userStories.description: As an AI agent debugging the side panel, I want to see the side panel's current React state and rendered HTML so that I can understand what the user sees.
acceptanceCriteria: Side panel bridge (App.tsx) handles incoming 'sp:getState' messages from the background script
Response includes rendered HTML: document.getElementById('root').innerHTML (truncated to 50000 chars)
Response includes React state snapshot: { connected, loading, pluginCount, plugins array }
Tool dispatches to extension via 'extension.getSidePanel' method
If the side panel is not open (no response within 3 seconds), returns { open: false }
If the side panel responds, returns { open: true, state: {...}, html: '...' }
userStories.notes:
The side panel runs in a separate extension page context. Communication uses chrome.runtime.sendMessage / onMessage.

The side panel already has a message listener in App.tsx that handles 'sp:connectionState' and 'sp:serverMessage'. Add a new case for 'sp:getState' that collects current React state and DOM innerHTML, then responds via sendResponse.

The React state is inside App component's hooks. Add the 'sp:getState' handler inside the existing useEffect that registers the onMessage listener — it has closure access to state variables.

For the background handler: send 'sp:getState' to side panel via chrome.runtime.sendMessage. Use a Promise with 3-second timeout.

Files to create/edit: (1) Edit side-panel/App.tsx — add 'sp:getState' handler, (2) Create browser-tools/extension-get-side-panel.ts, (3) Add handler to browser-commands.ts, (4) Add to message-router.ts + ALLOWED_METHODS + index.ts.

userStories.title: Add extension_check_adapter tool for plugin injection diagnostics
userStories.description: As an AI agent debugging why a plugin's tools are failing, I want to check the adapter injection status for a specific plugin across all matching tabs.
acceptanceCriteria: Tool accepts plugin (required — plugin name string)
Tool dispatches to extension via 'extension.checkAdapter' method
For each matching tab, handler checks globalThis.__openTabs.adapters[pluginName] and returns: tabId, tabUrl, adapterPresent, adapterHash, hashMatch, isReady, toolCount, toolNames
If plugin name not found in metadata, returns clear error
isReady() probe has 5-second timeout per tab
userStories.notes:
The handler needs to:
1. Look up plugin metadata via getPluginMeta(pluginName)
2. Find matching tabs: query chrome.tabs with the plugin's URL patterns
3. For each tab, run chrome.scripting.executeScript to inspect globalThis.__openTabs.adapters[pluginName]
4. Run isReady() with Promise.race timeout
5. Compare adapter hash to expected hash
6. Run tab checks in parallel (Promise.allSettled)

Files to create/edit: (1) Create browser-tools/extension-check-adapter.ts, (2) Add handler to browser-commands.ts, (3) Add to message-router.ts + ALLOWED_METHODS + index.ts.

userStories.title: Add extension_force_reconnect tool for soft WebSocket recovery
userStories.description: As an AI agent diagnosing connection issues, I want to force the WebSocket to disconnect and reconnect without reloading the entire extension.
acceptanceCriteria: Tool dispatches to extension via 'extension.forceReconnect' method
Handler sends response immediately (before WebSocket drops)
Handler then sends 'bg:forceReconnect' message to offscreen document
Offscreen document handles 'bg:forceReconnect' by closing WebSocket, resetting backoff, and reconnecting immediately
After reconnection, normal sync.full flow resumes
userStories.notes:
The force reconnect flow:
1. MCP server dispatches 'extension.forceReconnect' via WebSocket
2. Background handler sends JSON-RPC success response FIRST (before WebSocket drops)
3. Then sends 'bg:forceReconnect' to offscreen via chrome.runtime.sendMessage (with small setTimeout to let response flush)
4. Offscreen handler: close ws, reset backoffMs to 1000, call connect()

Files to create/edit: (1) Create browser-tools/extension-force-reconnect.ts, (2) Add handler to browser-commands.ts, (3) Add to message-router.ts + ALLOWED_METHODS + index.ts, (4) Add 'bg:forceReconnect' handler to offscreen/index.ts.

userStories.title: Add E2E tests for extension debugging tools
userStories.description: As a developer, I want E2E tests for the extension debugging tools so that state inspection, log retrieval, adapter diagnostics, and force reconnect are all verified through the full MCP pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: New test describe block 'Extension debugging tools' in browser-tools.e2e.ts
Test: extension_get_state returns state with connection.wsConnected=true and plugins array with e2e-test plugin
Test: extension_get_logs returns non-empty entries array with timestamp, level, source, message fields
Test: extension_get_side_panel returns { open: false } when side panel is not open
Test: extension_check_adapter returns adapter diagnostics for e2e-test plugin with adapterPresent=true
Test: extension_check_adapter returns error for non-existent plugin name
Test: extension_force_reconnect triggers reconnection — verify extension reconnects and tools still work after
BROWSER_TOOL_NAMES in helpers.ts updated to include all new tool names
userStories.notes:
The e2e-test plugin is already set up in fixtures and matches the test server URL pattern. Use setupToolTest or standard fixture pattern.

For extension_get_state: parameterless tool — call and verify response shape.
For extension_get_logs: background logs exist from startup — filter source='background'.
For extension_get_side_panel: side panel NOT open in Playwright — expect { open: false }.
For extension_check_adapter: open test server tab, wait for adapter injection, call check_adapter.
For extension_force_reconnect: call force_reconnect, poll waitForExtensionConnected, verify a tool still works.

All new tool names must be added to BROWSER_TOOL_NAMES in helpers.ts.
